label: Hidden Markov models & CRFs

node.label: Hidden Markov models & CRFs
node.definition: Hidden Markov models generate sequences through latent Markov state, whereas conditional random fields directly model structured output distributions conditioned on observations.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Hidden Markov models & CRFs is modeled as depending on Sequences, events & time series, Latent variables & hidden state and feeding downstream nodes such as Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection, Document intelligence & information extraction.
examples: atlas downstream link to Time-series forecasting, anomaly & fraud detection
atlas downstream link to Document intelligence & information extraction
limitations: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Sequences, events & time series, Latent variables & hidden state.

dependencies.rationale: Hidden Markov models & CRFs consumes, emits, or organizes information using Sequences, events & time series.
In this atlas graph, Hidden Markov models & CRFs has a `represents_with` dependency on Latent variables & hidden state with `core` strength.

In this atlas graph, Hidden Markov models & CRFs has an `optimizes_for` dependency on MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing because MCMC, EM & probabilistic message passing is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

In this atlas graph, Hidden Markov models & CRFs has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy because Maximum likelihood & cross-entropy is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
